hypothesis: If the chemically grounded evaluation framework is valid, then it will reliably distinguish high-quality generated crystal structures from perturbed or low-quality ones by showing that structures produced by diffusion models trained on MP/OQMD exhibit significantly lower DFT-relaxed RMSD, lower energy-above-hull (SUN ≤ 0.1 eV/atom), closer property-distribution matches (low EMD for density and formation energy) and higher latent precision/recall than perturbed controls, with all metric scores degrading monotonically as perturbation scale increases.
